title: Aureline per-service-family connectivity-state and snapshot record
description: Boundary schema for the per-service-family connectivity_state_record and the multi-family connectivity_state_snapshot_record every Aureline status surface, environment status strip, repair-action card, queue-review surface, support export, and admin reconciliation console reads. Connectivity is per-service-family: a single global online/offline toggle is non-conforming. See docs/runtime/connectivity_and_reconciliation_contract.md. Service-family, state, freshness, transport-posture, and continuity vocabularies are re-exported from the upstream contracts named in that document; this schema does not mint parallel names. The eventual outbox / reconciler crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary. Adding a new service_family_class, connectivity_state_class, or reauth_step_up_class is additive-minor and MUST bump connectivity_state_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

connectivity_state_schema_version.description: Integer schema version. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, support bundles, and admin exports. Raw URLs and raw token bodies never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing posture or rationale. Raw secret material and raw payload bytes MUST NOT appear.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

service_family_class.description: Frozen service-family vocabulary. Connectivity is computed per family; a surface MAY NOT invent a family outside this set.

connectivity_state_class.description: Frozen per-family connectivity-state vocabulary. Surfaces that cannot place a family in this matrix MUST use service_unavailable; not_applicable is reserved for families the deployment profile does not claim.

reauth_step_up_class.description: Step-up class required to clear reauth_required. Required on the reauth_required state.

since_at.description: When the current state was entered. Null is admissible only when state is connected or not_applicable.

freshness_floor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the freshness floor the state was computed under. Required on constrained, reconciliation_pending, and service_unavailable.

transport_posture_ref.description: Opaque ref to the transport_posture_record the state was derived from. Required on every state other than not_applicable.

local_core_continuity_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the local_core_continuity_packet_record this state composes with. Required when state is offline_local_safe, service_unavailable, or constrained for the local_core / cached_reads families.

reauth_step_up_class.description: Required when state is reauth_required; null otherwise.

allOf.description: since_at: null is admissible only on connected or not_applicable states.
freshness_floor_ref MUST be non-null on constrained, reconciliation_pending, and service_unavailable.
transport_posture_ref MUST be non-null on every state other than not_applicable.
reauth_step_up_class MUST be set when state is reauth_required.
local_core_continuity_packet_ref MUST be non-null when state is offline_local_safe or service_unavailable on any family, or when the family is local_core / cached_reads on a constrained state.

family_states.description: One inner connectivity_state_record per applicable service family. Tooling MUST reject duplicate family entries; the JSON Schema boundary here only pins row shape.
